PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jan-02-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:

Bruce Feder, Esq., via Zoom, for Jewish Family and Children's Services, Petitioner.

At the outset, Mr. Feder reports regarding the efforts to determine heirs in this matter and confirms of no known objections. Upon conclusion of testimony, the court finds that the heir search in this matter is satisfactory. No one appeared to object. There being no objections, the Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary. The will dated Dec-01-2021 and codicil dated Feb-28-2022 are admitted to probate. Jewish Family and Children's Services is appointed Executor of the estate with full authority. Bond is not required. Shirley Yawitz is appointed probate Referee. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for Jan-07-2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is filed two-weeks prior, the status hearing will be taken off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Jaime/Reed. Not Reported.
